prompt:
Platform ticket. Our order service already writes side-effect intents (partner syncs, email sends) into an outbox table in the same transaction as the business write — but the thing draining that table toward the delivery sink is a cron'd shell script with zero guarantees. Build the real dispatcher as a library. Module go-outbox, package outbox, stdlib only. No goroutines needed inside, but every method must be safe under concurrent callers — we run -race in CI.

Types:
- Record: ID, Key, Payload (strings), Attempts (int), State (string: "pending", "delivered", "parked"), Reason (string, why a record was parked), NextAttemptAt (time.Time), Seq (int — insertion sequence, starts at 1).
- Sink interface { Deliver(r Record) error } — the tests inject a scripted fake.
- Options: Now func() time.Time (nil → time.Now), MaxAttempts int (<=0 → 5), BaseDelay time.Duration (<=0 → time.Second), MaxDelay time.Duration (<=0 → time.Minute).
- Result: Delivered, Retried, Parked — []string of record IDs in processing order. Always non-nil, even when empty.

API and semantics:
- New(sink Sink, opts Options) *Dispatcher.
- Add(id, key, payload string) error — duplicate id or empty id/key → error. New records: State pending, Attempts 0, NextAttemptAt zero (due immediately), next Seq.
- Dispatch() Result — one draining pass, now sampled once from opts.Now at the start. A record is DUE when it is pending, NextAttemptAt is not after now, and it is the oldest (lowest Seq) pending record for its Key. Per-key ordering is the entire point: while an older record of a key stays pending, younger records of that key wait — even when the older one is just sitting out a retry backoff. Other keys are unaffected. Scan in Seq order and keep scanning until nothing more is due, because a success or a parking can unblock the next record of the same key within the same call; but never attempt the same record twice in one Dispatch.
- Every sink call increments Attempts, success or not. Sink returns nil → State delivered. A record is marked delivered ONLY on a nil return. If the sink actually got the message out but returned an error anyway (timeouts happen), we WILL send it again — at-least-once is the contract here, dedupe belongs to the receiving side.
- Sink returns an error: if Attempts (just incremented) has reached MaxAttempts, the record parks — State parked, Reason = err.Error() — and the key's next pending record becomes eligible, because a parked record no longer blocks its key. Otherwise schedule the retry: NextAttemptAt = now + BaseDelay*2^(Attempts-1), capped at MaxDelay.
- Snapshot() []Record — copies of every record (delivered and parked included, it doubles as the audit view), ascending Seq.
- Load(records []Record, sink Sink, opts Options) (*Dispatcher, error) — adopt a snapshot from a previous process; this is the restart path. Seq is reassigned 1..n in slice order; duplicate ids or an unknown State → error. Pending records will simply be attempted again — that's the at-least-once trade on crash: anything not yet marked delivered gets redelivered.
- Retry(id string) error — ops resubmits a parked record: back to pending, Attempts 0, Reason cleared, due immediately. Unknown id or a record that isn't parked → error.

Acceptance contract: outbox_test.go, and `go test -race -timeout 120s ./...` must pass. Don't touch the test file.
